join_order.build: customer(filtered c_mktsegment=1, ~300K rows) → bitset on c_custkey
join_order.probe: orders(zone-map pruned o_orderdate < 9204, ~7.3M rows scanned, filter o_orderdate < 9204 + probe custkey bitset)
join_order.rationale: customer is smallest filtered relation; bitset on custkey fits L2 cache (187KB), making probe O(1) with zero cache miss

join_order.build: qualifying_orders(~1.47M rows) → compact_hash_map on o_orderkey, storing {o_orderdate, o_shippriority}; simultaneously populate bloom_filter on o_orderkey
join_order.probe: lineitem(zone-map pruned l_shipdate > 9204, ~30.6M rows) → bloom pre-filter → hash map probe → aggregate
join_order.rationale: orders hash map ~17MB fits in L3 (88MB); bloom filter (1.47M keys, ~1.8MB) pre-filters ~90% of non-matching lineitem rows before touching hash map

data_structures.custkey_filter: bitset(capacity=1500001 bits, ~187KB) — direct bit test on int32_t c_custkey; fits L2 cache, O(1) set/test with no hash overhead
data_structures.orders_join: compact_hash_map(open_addressing, pre_sized capacity=3000000) keyed on int32_t o_orderkey → struct{int32_t o_orderdate; int32_t o_shippriority}; ~36MB total, fits L3 (88MB)
data_structures.orders_bloom: bloom_filter(1.47M keys, 10 bits/key, ~1.84MB) built simultaneously with orders hash map; used to pre-filter lineitem rows; ~1% false positive rate
data_structures.aggregation: compact_hash_map(open_addressing, pre_sized capacity=8192) keyed on int32_t l_orderkey → struct{double revenue; int32_t o_orderdate; int32_t o_shippriority}; one per thread (~3K groups expected)

phases.description: parallel scan of c_mktsegment (1 byte/row) and c_custkey (4 bytes/row); threads collect qualifying custkeys into thread-local vectors; sequential bitset population after barrier
phases.note: customer is 1.5MB total — fast even single-threaded; parallelize for consistency

phases.description: parallel morsel scan of o_orderdate + o_custkey + o_orderkey + o_shippriority; per block skip if block_min >= 9204 (zone map); per row: check o_orderdate < 9204 AND bitset[o_custkey]; collect qualifying rows thread-locally; sequential build of orders compact_hash_map and bloom_filter after barrier

parallel morsel scan of l_shipdate + l_orderkey + l_extendedprice + l_discount; per block skip if block_max <= 9204 (zone map); per row: check l_shipdate > 9204; bloom pre-filter on l_orderkey; probe orders hash map; accumulate revenue into thread-local agg map; merge thread-local maps after barrier
phases.bloom_benefit: ~90% of non-matching l_orderkeys eliminated before hash map probe

phases.description: after merging 64 thread-local agg maps (~3K total groups), run std::partial_sort for top-10 by (revenue DESC, o_orderdate ASC); format o_orderdate as YYYY-MM-DD string for output

index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=150] then per block [int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t block_nrows]
index_usage.purpose: skip rightmost ~77 of 150 blocks; reduces orders scan from 15M to ~7.3M rows (~51% I/O reduction)
index_usage.row_offset_note: row_offset is ROW index into column arrays, not byte offset

index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=600] then per block [int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t block_nrows]
index_usage.purpose: skip first ~294 of 600 blocks; reduces lineitem scan from 60M to ~30.6M rows (~49% I/O reduction)
index_usage.row_offset_note: row_offset is ROW index into column arrays, not byte offset

topk.comparator: primary: revenue DESC (double), secondary: o_orderdate ASC (int32_t)
topk.complexity: O(n log k) where n=3000, k=10 — trivial cost

key_optimizations: bitset on c_custkey (187KB, L2-resident) replaces hash set — zero hash overhead, branchless test
bloom filter on qualifying o_orderkeys (~1.84MB, L3-resident) eliminates ~90% of lineitem→orders hash probes
zone-map pruning halves I/O on both orders (~51%) and lineitem (~49%)
thread-local aggregation maps avoid all synchronization during hot lineitem scan loop
3K agg groups fit entirely in L1 cache per thread — near-zero cache miss on agg inserts
single-pass fused scan: shipdate filter + bloom check + hash probe + revenue accumulate in one loop

phase_breakdown.orders_scan_filter_build_hashmap_bloom: ~15ms (7.3M effective rows, 4 cols × 4 bytes, 64 threads, zone-map skip ~51%)
phase_breakdown.lineitem_scan_probe_aggregate: ~55ms (30.6M effective rows, 4 cols avg 6 bytes, 64 threads, zone-map skip ~49%, bloom pre-filter ~90% skip)
phase_breakdown.merge_and_topk: ~2ms (64 × 3K agg entries merged, partial_sort on 3K groups)

estimated_cost.bottleneck: lineitem column I/O and hash map probes (orders HT ~36MB fits L3, minimizing cache misses)
